rule: Reject any provider or mock that cannot keep `partial`, `ready`, `failed`, and `superseded` transcript states explicit.
Reject any scan provider that cannot represent `clean`, `suspicious`, `quarantined`, `unreadable`, and `failed` outcomes separately.
Reject any live transcription provider whose callbacks cannot be treated as hints followed by trusted state re-fetch or whose deletion posture is unclear.
Reject any scan provider that cannot route unsafe or unreadable artifacts into explicit quarantine and fallback review.
Reject any provider whose official product posture warns against entering sensitive or personal information without a stronger healthcare contract path.
Reject any automatic single-suite winner if it couples transcript and scan failure domains more tightly than the adapter contract requires.
Reject any provider where scan completion implies safety or readability without a separate Vecells readiness and quarantine decision.
Reject any provider that makes transcript completion look like clinical usability instead of derived evidence awaiting readiness assessment.
Reject threat-intel-oriented services when portability, file handling, or quarantine orchestration are weaker than the shortlist alternatives.
Reject any provider whose onboarding burden or cloud coupling would force seq_035 to choose storage, identity, and vendor stack together before the product contract is frozen.
